data.title: Quantum Computing to raise $50M via stock offering
data.description:
• Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT) entered into securities purchase agreements with institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 1.54M shares at a purchase price of $5.00 per share.
  • In a concurrent private placement, the company entered into securities purchase agreements for the purchase and sale of an additional 8.46M shares at the same price.
  • The offerings are expected to result in aggregate gross proceeds of $50M.
  • The closing of the offerings is expected to occur on or about December 12, 2024.
  • Net proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The tsock price declined 6.4% on Wednesday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: GME, GEV, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were mixed during the premarket hours of Wednesday as investors eagerly awaited key inflation data set to be released later today, a pivotal economic indicator that could influence future Federal Reserve policy.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • GameStop (GME) shares climbed over 4% despite mixed FQ3 results, with revenue plunging over 20% Y/Y. The company closed the quarter with $4.62B in cash, significantly higher than $929.2M a year ago. CEO Matt Furlong noted, "We remain focused on right-sizing our cost structure while investing in growth initiatives." The company also stated it does not plan any further at-the-market stock offerings for the current fiscal year.

Biggest stock losers

  • GE Vernova (GEV) shares dropped 3% after the company reaffirmed its FY 2024 guidance but narrowed its adjusted EBITDA margin outlook to 5.5%-6.0% from the previous 5%-7%, raising investor concerns despite revenues and free cash flow trending toward the higher end of prior guidance. While the company raised its FY 2025 forecasts for revenues ($36B-$37B), free cash flow ($2B-$2.5B), and adjusted EBITDA margin (high single digits), these updates failed to ease concerns as revenue guidance remained below the $37.3B analyst consensus. The company also declared a $0.25/share quarterly dividend and approved an initial $6B share repurchase authorization.
  • Dave & Buster’s Entertainment (PLAY) shares tumbled 15% following a downbeat FQ3, with a wider-than-expected loss. The company's profitability was impacted by a material fiscal calendar mismatch, adverse weather, remodel construction, and certain "unusual" items. Adding to the pressure, CEO Chris Morris announced his resignation. The board appointed Chairman Kevin Sheehan as interim CEO.

data.title: G2 seeks 10 for 1 share consolidation
data.description:
• G2 Energy (OTCPK:GTGEF) will be seeking the Canadian Securities Exchange approval to consolidate all of its issued and outstanding common shares on the basis  of 10:1, with each ten pre-consolidated shares being consolidated into one post-consolidated share.  
  • Shareholder approval will not be required for the share consolidation, while it has been approved by the company’s board of directors.

data.title: PureWave Hydrogen appoints Tatenda Muhle as CFO
data.description:
• PureWave Hydrogen (TSXV:PWH:CA) announces the appointment of Tatenda Muhle to succeed Ryan Cheung as its new CFO, effective as of December 6, 2024.
  • Cheung has decided to step down from the position to pursue other professional opportunities. However, he will remain with the company through a transition period to ensure a seamless handover of all accounting responsibilities.
  • Muhle, with extensive experience in financial leadership, strategic planning, and corporate governance, has held senior roles with other public and private companies.

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX valued at $350B in latest tender offer - report
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX (SPACE) and its investors have agreed to buy as much as $1.25B of insider shares in a deal that values the spacecraft maker at about $350B, Bloomberg reported, citing an internal email.

SpaceX (SPACE) alone is offering to buy as much as $500M worth of stock in the tender offer. The $350B valuation makes it the world's most valuable private startup.

The deal's price of $185/share is significantly higher than SpaceX's (SPACE) tender offer in September, which was priced at $112 apiece. The company had proposed pricing its shares at about $125 as recently as mid-November.

The high investor appetite comes as Musk's proximity to president-elect Donald Trump brought on a so-called "Trump bump" in the billionaire's businesses. Musk's wealth has swelled to about $384B, according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index, up $155B this year.

Musk's AI startup xAI reached a valuation of $50B following its funding round last month. Meanwhile, Tesla's (TSLA) stock has soared close to 60% since Election Day, with its market cap currently at $1.26T.

data.title: Microsoft shareholders vote no on bitcoin despite Michael Saylor's pitch
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) shareholders have rejected a proposal by conservative think tank National Center for Public Policy Research and Michael Saylor, co-founder of MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), to explore diversifying its balance sheet with bitcoin (BTC-USD).

"Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) can't afford to miss the next Technology Wave," Saylor said in a presentation at the tech giant's annual shareholder meeting. "Bitcoin is the Next Wave."

"You can convert your cash flows and your dividends and your buybacks and your debt into bitcoin. If you do that, you’ll add hundreds of dollars to the stock price."

Saylor claimed that bitcoin (BTC-USD) has the potential to contribute $155-$584 per share to Microsoft's (MSFT) stock price while reducing shareholder risk, and create between $1T and $4.9T in shareholder value.

According to the proxy statement for Microsoft's (MSFT) shareholder meeting, the proposal for exploring investing in bitcoin stated: "At minimum, companies should evaluate the benefits of holding some, even just 1%, of its assets in bitcoin."

Microsoft's (MSFT) board recommended that shareholders reject the proposal, pointing to crypto volatility, but said it continues to monitor developments and trends in the space.

Saylor has turned MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) into the world's largest corporate bitcoin (BTC-USD) holder, with 423,650 BTC valued at $41.45B (2% of BTC's total supply).

data.title: European markets in red ahead of ECB decision and U.S. inflation
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.33%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.17%

France (CAC:IND) -0.18%

Slovakia’s construction output dropped by 4.4% Y/Y in October 2024.

Romania’s annual inflation rate increased to 5.1% in November.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.23% lower, as markets await key US inflation numbers. Markets bracing for policy decisions from the European Central Bank dur tomorrow and the Swiss National Bank later in the week.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on economic front.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.23%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 2.11%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.31%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Performant Financial announces name change to Performant Healthcare to focus on healthcare sector
data.description:
• Performant Healthcare Inc., formerly known as Performant Financial (NASDAQ:PFMT) announced a corporate name change effective Monday, to focus on the healthcare sector.
  • The company also revealed plans to transition its common stock to a new ticker symbol, "PHLT," replacing the previous "PFMT."
  • Performant Healthcare's strategic rebranding and transition to the "PHLT" ticker symbol is anticipated to enhance the company's branding and market recognition within the healthcare services industry.

data.title: Canfor buys additional 7% in Sweden's Vida AB
data.description:
• Canfor (TSX:CFP:CA) acquired 7% of outstanding shares of VIDA AB, Sweden's largest privately owned sawmill company.
  • The shares were acquired from certain minority shareholders utilizing their option privileges under the February 2019 agreement in which Canfor purchased 70% of VIDA AB.
  • After concluding this transaction, Canfor owns 77% of VIDA AB.

data.title: Guardian Metal Resources reports agreement to purchase Pilot Mountain water rights
data.description:
• Guardian Metal Resources (OTCQX:GMTLF) on Wednesday said that its 100% owned flagship Pilot Mountain Project, located in the Walker Lane Mineral Belt in Nevada, has purchased water rights as it progresses toward the goal of supporting domestic U.S. production of tungsten.
  • A definitive water rights purchase and sale agreement has been executed between Pilot Metals, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Guardian Metal, and Belmont Resources for water Permit 87850 located in the Kibby Basin Valley, Nevada, which is directly adjacent to the company's Pilot Mountain Project.
  • The purchase price for Permit 87850 includes $350,000 which has been paid to Belmont, and a further $200,000, which is payable at completion on or before 31 January 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
